---
title: "4.13 Rotate a Row in a 2D Array"
subtitle: "Implementing 2D Array Algorithms"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Rotate and Reverse

### Row or column algorithms

We can also rotate or reverse the order of the elements in a row or column.

The following source code rotates the elements in a row to the right by one position.

This algorithm is very similar to the 1D array rotation algorithm because we are dealing with a single row or column.

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: rotate2DArray`

Create a method `rotateRowRight` that rotates the elements in a row to the right by one position.

It should return the rotated array.

## Rotate Starter

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class Rotate
{
     public static int[][] rotateRowRight(int[][] nums, int row)
     {
          int last = nums[row][nums[0].length - 1];
          // Write a backwards loop through the row's columns
          //  to copy elements at col-1 to col


          // Copy the last element to the first element at col 0


          return nums;
     }

     public static void main(String[] args)
     {
          int[][] matrix = { {3, 2, 3}, {4, 3, 6}, {8, 9, 3}, {10, 3, 3}};
          int[][] rotated = rotateRowRight(matrix, 1);
          for (int row = 0; row < rotated.length; row++)
          {
            for (int col = 0; col < rotated[0].length; col++)
            {
                 System.out.print(rotated[row][col] + " ");
            }
            System.out.println();
          }
     }
}
```
:::

## Rotate Test Target

### Main output

The output from `main` should be:

```text
3 2 3
6 4 3
8 9 3
10 3 3
```

The method call in `main` rotates row index `1`.

## Rotate Test Target

### Extra method call

A second test calls `rotateRowRight` with:

```java
int[][] array = { {1, 4, 8}, {6, 7, 9}};
Rotate.rotateRowRight(array, 0);
```

Expected output from the returned array:

```text
8 1 4
6 7 9
```

## Algorithm Focus

### Rotate one row right

The starter stores:

```java
int last = nums[row][nums[0].length - 1];
```

Then the missing loop should copy each value at `col - 1` into `col`, moving from right to left.

After the loop, copy `last` into column `0`.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

- store the last element before overwriting row values
- traverse the row from right to left
- copy `nums[row][col - 1]` into `nums[row][col]`
- place the saved value into `nums[row][0]`
- return the same `int[][]` after rotating the row

## End

### 4.13 Rotate a Row in a 2D Array

Next: Searching Algorithms.
